f2p_gates.description: GeminiTranscript struct in agent/geminicli/transcript.go has a field tagged `json:"projectHash"` (allowing ,omitempty); Go comments stripped before grep.
GeminiTranscript struct also has a field tagged `json:"startTime"` (allowing ,omitempty); the second new field exposed by the canonical patch.
strategy/manual_commit_rewind.go imports "encoding/json" — load-bearing for json.Unmarshal of transcript bytes; admits named imports.

manual_commit_rewind.go calls json.Unmarshal AND has a struct (named or anonymous) with both projectHash and startTime json tags — implementation-agnostic on helper name (canonical: extractGeminiMeta).

Both restore call sites gate on AgentTypeGemini (≥2 occurrences in file) AND a filepath.Join call references both ".gemini" and "chats" path segments AND a .ProjectHash field is dereferenced — the headline behavioral landmark.
Filename helper presents the Gemini-specific shape: contains Go time-format "2006-01-02T15-04", a "session-" prefix string, and an 8-char short-id truncation (slice [:8] OR len > 8 check).
Both ResolveSessionFilePath call sites are reached only via an `} else {` block (≥2 of ≥2 calls guarded) — proves the Gemini gating refactor preserves the legacy fallback for non-Gemini agents.

p2p_gates.description: go build ./... succeeds (informational only per CLAUDE.md — never zeros reward; both buggy and canonical states pass build).

previous_curator_log.notes: Original curator KEEP-d the SWE-rebench stub on the basis that the canonical patch added no new test functions, so an empty FAIL_TO_PASS was 'correct'. In practice this collapsed to test.sh's `if not fail_to_pass: reward = passed/total` fallback, which scored ≥0.99 across all v0.4.3 trials — pure noise. Replaced with a behavioral structural verifier.

notes: Rewrote SWE-rebench stub (empty FAIL_TO_PASS → 'overall pass rate' fallback scored ≥0.99 as pure noise across all v0.4.3 trials — degenerate ceiling). New tests/test.sh + tests/test_manifest.yaml install 7 weighted F2P structural gates (sum=1.00) targeting the canonical patch (data-pipeline/artifacts_cli/canonical_patches/e5a72190-7808-4cc0-9b23-1fe10104ea1e.json, fidelity=exact, 7 mutating ops, 2 files, +73/-3): (1) GeminiTranscript.ProjectHash json tag, (2) GeminiTranscript.StartTime json tag, (3) encoding/json import in manual_commit_rewind.go, (4) json.Unmarshal-based meta extractor with both projectHash + startTime tags, (5) AgentTypeGemini gating at ≥2 sites with .gemini/tmp/<projectHash>/chats path construction (headline), (6) session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<shortid> filename format with 8-char truncation, (7) both legacy ResolveSessionFilePath calls guarded inside `} else {` blocks. Validated locally: clone https://github.com/entireio/cli at base 1b69ae14da4a0d3deb08922bb6798b9bc5fb6e02; run test.sh against buggy baseline → reward 0.000; apply canonical patch → reward 1.000; preludes-only partial fix (struct fields + json import + meta helper, no path override) → reward 0.550. Anti-overfit: G1/G2 admit ,omitempty variations; G3 admits named imports; G4 admits anonymous-struct extractors with the right json tags; G5 admits any AgentTypeGemini equality form; G6 admits both `[:8]` and `len > 8` short-id idioms; G7 just requires the gating-refactor topology, not specific variable names. Go line and block comments are stripped before all grepping (no comment-spoof). P2P_GO_BUILD is informational only (p2p_failed=False always per CLAUDE.md scoring rules). Reward formula is weighted-replace, naturally bounded to [0, 1].
